# Ask Pulse Visualization Extreme Smoke Catalog - 1250 Questions

> **Status:** Scenario and question catalog, created 2026-05-26.
>
> **Scope:** Ask Pulse only. Visualization-generation stress questions only. No AI Insights, Dashboard, Settings, or BI-vendor embed cases.
>
> **Intent:** Give Claude a ready-to-run catalog for an extreme Ask Pulse visualization smoke test. Every row is a concrete prompt asking for a chart, graph, map, diagram, or visual artifact. Runtime execution is deferred until Rajesh asks to run it.

## Claude Instructions

Use this file when Rajesh asks Claude to run the Ask Pulse visualization smoke. Do not execute it while merely preparing the catalog.

1. Run `python scripts/llm_onboard.py --terse` before touching code or running the UI.
2. Confirm the target profile: `smoke-fixture` for UI/render speed, or a live profile such as Genie/Foundation/Supervisor for real backend behavior.
3. Open Ask Pulse only: `/?surface=ask-pulse`. Do not mix AI Insights or Dashboard cases into this run.
4. Submit each prompt exactly as written in the `Claude prompt` column.
5. For each case, capture answer first paint, visual artifact first paint, completed terminal state, chart/table/SQL/evidence tab status, screenshot on failure, console errors, page errors, and failed `/api/*` responses.
6. If Ask Pulse cannot produce the requested chart type, the acceptable behavior is an honest fallback: clear limitation plus best available table or simpler chart. A blank chart, stale chart, stuck spinner, or invented unsupported capability is a failure.
7. Fix continuously only when the root cause is inside PulsePlay. Do not patch around an upstream Genie limitation, missing live credentials, or unsupported chart family; record those honestly.
8. After each fix, rerun the exact failing `APV-*` case before continuing.
9. Save evidence under `docs/evidence/ask-pulse-visual-1250-YYYY-MM-DD/` and update `docs/HANDOVER.md`, `docs/memory/project_state.md`, and `docs/AGENDA.md` before closing.

## Timing And Verdict Fields

| Field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `caseId` | `APV-0001` through `APV-1250` |
| `profile` | AI profile used for the run |
| `submitAt` | Timestamp immediately before submit |
| `userBubbleMs` | Time until user prompt appears in the thread |
| `firstAssistantPaintMs` | Time until any assistant text, progress, blocked state, or fallback appears |
| `visualFirstPaintMs` | Time until chart, map, diagram, or visual placeholder first appears |
| `artifactTabsMs` | Time until chart/table/SQL/evidence/reasoning tabs are usable, if present |
| `completedMs` | Time until completed, failed, blocked, or terminal fallback state |
| `verdict` | `PASS`, `FAIL`, `SKIP-ENV`, `UNSUPPORTED-OK`, or `NEEDS-REVIEW` |
| `issueClass` | `chart-render`, `layout-overflow`, `stale-artifact`, `unsupported-visual`, `data-contract`, `backend-env`, `performance`, `accessibility`, `test-design` |
| `notes` | Short honest observation |

## Execution Bands

| Band | Cases | Purpose |
|---|---:|---|
| First probe | 25 | Validate runner selectors, Ask Pulse submit path, and evidence capture. |
| Visual family sweep | 100 | Two cases per visual family, enough to reveal unsupported families quickly. |
| Domain sweep | 250 | Ten cases per domain across many visual types. |
| Heavy smoke | 500 | Half-catalog run for performance and layout stability. |
| Extreme smoke | 1250 | Full catalog; only run when evidence storage and time budget are ready. |

## Pass Rules

- `PASS`: Ask Pulse renders the requested visual or a clearly equivalent visual, with readable labels, no stale artifacts, no blank chart, and no unhandled console/page errors.
- `UNSUPPORTED-OK`: Ask Pulse says the exact chart family is unsupported and provides a truthful fallback chart or table.
- `FAIL`: blank visual, broken canvas, clipped controls, incorrect stale data after another prompt, stuck spinner, invented data/capability, or UI crash.
- `SKIP-ENV`: live backend, credentials, certificates, or network conditions block the case before the visualization path can be tested.
- `NEEDS-REVIEW`: visual renders but correctness or readability needs human inspection.

## Question Catalog

| ID | Visual target | Domain | Stressor | Claude prompt | Primary visual check |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| APV-0001 | Grouped bar chart | finance revenue operations | include missing periods as visible gaps and explain how the visual handles incomplete data | [Instruction for Claude:] Take the following question and generate the most appropriate, detailed, and complex visualization possible using Ask Pulse. [User Question:] Show finance revenue operations using a grouped bar chart using revenue, cost, margin, forecast variance, business unit, quarter; compare grouped bars across peer categories with sorted ranking; include missing periods as visible gaps and explain how the visual handles incomplete data; use a blue-orange diverging palette; include hover tooltips with raw value, normalized value, and source slice; add a concise visual insight title and a table fallback if the chart type is unsupported. | grouped bars render, zero baseline is visible, labels do not overlap |
| APV-0002 | Grouped bar chart | retail sales performance | highlight severe outliers with callouts and keep the axis readable | [Instruction for Claude:] Take the following question and generate the most appropriate, detailed, and complex visualization possible using Ask Pulse. [User Question:] Build an Ask Pulse grouped bar chart for retail sales performance using sales, profit, discount, basket size, store, category, region; compare grouped bars across peer categories with sorted ranking; highlight severe outliers with callouts and keep the axis readable; use a color-blind safe categorical palette; include legend toggles for noisy series; add a concise visual insight title and a table fallback if the chart type is unsupported. | grouped bars render, zero baseline is visible, labels do not overlap |
| APV-0003 | Grouped bar chart | e-commerce conversion funnel | handle a highly skewed long-tail distribution without hiding small categories | [Instruction for Claude:] Take the following question and generate the most appropriate, detailed, and complex visualization possible using Ask Pulse. [User Question:] Visualize e-commerce conversion funnel with a grouped bar chart using sessions, add-to-cart, checkout, conversion rate, channel, device, week; compare grouped bars across peer categories with sorted ranking; handle a highly skewed long-tail distribution without hiding small categories; use a monochrome print-safe palette; include drilldown chips from summary to detail; add a concise visual insight title and a table fallback if the chart type is unsupported. | grouped bars render, zero baseline is visible, labels do not overlap |
| APV-0004 | Grouped bar chart | sports team performance | work with only three data points and state whether the visual is still appropriate | [Instruction for Claude:] Take the following question and generate the most appropriate, detailed, and complex visualization possible using Ask Pulse. [User Question:] Generate a grouped bar chart that explains sports team performance using wins, expected points, player load, shot quality, team, season, match; compare grouped bars across peer categories with sorted ranking; work with only three data points and state whether the visual is still appropriate; use a traffic-light thresholds with text labels; include reference line and benchmark marker; add a concise visual insight title and a table fallback if the chart type is unsupported. | grouped bars render, zero baseline is visible, labels do not overlap |
